prevalence.note: ONS COVID Infection Survey estimates of self-reported long COVID (4+ weeks symptoms)

economicImpact.note: Estimated economic output lost from long COVID-related work absence and reduced productivity

sources.name: ONS
sources.dataset: COVID Infection Survey — prevalence of ongoing symptoms following COVID-19

sources.name: NIHR
sources.dataset: Long COVID research and clinical data

metadata.methodology: Prevalence from ONS COVID Infection Survey — people self-reporting symptoms 4+ weeks after COVID-19 infection. Economic impact is ONS/HMT estimates of output loss from work absence and reduced productivity. NHS clinic data from NHS England Long COVID assessment service statistics.
knownIssues: ONS survey discontinued in 2023 — later figures are modelled estimates
Long COVID has no standard clinical definition across all studies
Prevalence likely understated as many do not seek medical help
Economic impact figures are estimates with wide confidence intervals
